title: Device Translation Table Mnemonics
text: Device Translation Table Mnemonics Uniface uses a set of mnemonics to identify particular functions. When you define a device translation table, you define the device control codes that implement a particular function. Uniface ignores the case of the mnemonics, that is, uppercase and lowercase are equivalent. An single caret (^) can be used in front of a mnemonic to improve the readability of a device translation table. Not all commands are supported by all devices. In fact, some commands are only valid for printers but not terminals and the other way around. Table 1. Device Control Functions Mnemonic Section Explanation Details begin_device Device Mark start of general device information. Device Section begin_font Font Mark start of font definition. Font Set Section begin_set Character Set Mark start of character set definition. Character Set Section begin_table Delimiter Mark start of the device table. Device Translation Table Syntax bli_hig Device Set video to blink and high intensity. Secondary Process Color and Video Mnemonics bli_hig_inv Device Set video to blink, high intensity, and inverse. bli_hig_inv_und Device Set video to blink, high intensity, inverse, and underscore. bli_hig_und Device Set video to blink, high intensity, and underscore. bli_inv Device Set video to blink and inverse. bli_inv_und Device Set video to blink, inverse, and underscore. bli_und Device Set video to blink and underscore. blink Device Set video to blink. _blink Device Set video to blink in graphics mode. Secondary Process Color and Video Mnemonics bol_ita Device, Character Set Set shape to bold and italics. Character Shape Mnemonics bol_ita_und Device, Character Set Set shape to bold, italics, and underline. bol_und Device, Character Set Set shape to bold and underline. bold (or bol) Device, Character Set Set shape to bold. bot_con Character Set Border bottom connector. Border Character Mnemonics bot_left Character Set Border bottom left corner. bot_line Character Set Border bottom line. bot_right Character Set Border bottom right corner. buzz Device Give a beep. Sound Mnemonic clear_screen Device Clear the entire screen. Screen Clearing Mnemonics close Device Return the device to non-Uniface state. Session Mnemonics _close Device Reset device from graphics mode. Graphics Mode Mnemonics color Device General color calculate command. Graphics Mode Mnemonics color_ n Device Set to color combination n (0 through 63). _color Device Colors for secondary process. Secondary Process Color and Video Mnemonics cursor Device Position the cursor. Cursor Positioning Mnemonics _cursor Device Position the cursor in graphics mode. end_device Device Mark end of general device information. Device Section end_font Font Mark end of font definition. Font Set Section end_set Set Mark end of character set definition. Character Set Section end_table Delimiter Mark end of the device table. Device Translation Table Syntax erase_line Device Erase a part of line. Screen Clearing Mnemonics euc Character Set Define the EUC (Japanese) character set. Double-Byte Character Set Mnemonics formfeed Device Scroll to the top of the screen. Print Control Mnemonics gb2312 Character Set Define the GB2312 (simplified Chinese) character set. Double-Byte Character Set Mnemonics hig_inv Device Set video to high intensity and inverse. Video Mode Mnemonics hig_inv_und Device Set video to high intensity, inverse, and underscore. hig_und Device Set video to high intensity and underscore. high Device Set video to high intensity. _high Device Set video to high intensity in graphics mode. Secondary Process Color and Video Mnemonics init Device Initialize, for example, select keypad, then clear screen. Session Mnemonics _init Device Initialize device in graphics mode. Graphics Mode Mnemonics inv_und Device Set video to inverse and underscore. Video Mode Mnemonics inverse Device Set video to inverse. _inverse Device Set video to inverse in graphics mode. Secondary Process Color and Video Mnemonics ita_und Device Character Set Set shape to italics and underline. Character Shape Mnemonics italic (or ita) Device Character Set Set shape to italics. ksc5601 Character Set Define the KSC5601 (Korean) character set. Double-Byte Character Set Mnemonics linefeed Device Line feed. Print Control Mnemonics meta_ Variant Device External character set of the translation table Meta_Variant mid_con Character Set Border middle connector. Border Character Mnemonics mid_left Character Set Border middle left corner. mid_line Character Set Border middle line. mid_right Character Set Border middle right corner. mode_ n Device Specify the dimensions for mode n (0 through 7). Dimension Mnemonics normal (or nor) Device Reset to normal shape. Video Mode Mnemonics novideo Reset to normal video. null Device Character Set Font Delimitor Null Mnemonics printer_inverse Character Set Character used with _inverse, default is ‘.’. Secondary Process Character Mnemonics printer_underscore Character Set Character used with _underscore, default is ‘_’. reset Character Set Shift out of the current character set. Character Set Selection Mnemonics return Device Carriage return (without line feed). Print Control Mnemonics scroll_down Device Scrolling functions; shift a section of the screen. Scrolling Mnemonics scroll_left Device scroll_right Device scroll_up Device select Character Set Shift into the character set being defined. Character Set Selection Mnemonics sjis Character Set Define the SJIS (shift Japanese) character set. Double-Byte Character Set Mnemonics tca Character Set Define the TCA (traditional Chinese) character set. top_con Character Set Border top connector. Border Character Mnemonics top_left Character Set Border top left corner. top_line Character Set Border top line. top_right Character Set Border top right corner. underline (or und) Device Character Set Set shape to underline. Character Shape Mnemonics underscore Character Set Set video to underscore. Video Mode Mnemonics _underscore Device Set video to underscore in graphics mode. Character Set Selection Mnemonics vert_left Character Set Border left vertical. Border Character Mnemonics vert_mid Character Set Border middle vertical. vert_right Character Set Border right vertical. view_bot Character Set Entity bottom line in View mode. View Mode Character Mnemonics view_cc Character Set Copyright character. Copyright and Menu Mnemonics view_check Character Set Appearance of a check box when on in character mode. Character Mode Widget Mnemonics view_check_null Character Set Appearance of a tri-state check box when null in character mode. view_check_off Character Set Appearance of a check box when off in character mode. view_cr Character Set Carriage return character in View mode. View Mode Character Mnemonics view_dropdown Character Set Drop-down list indicator in character mode. Character Mode Widget Mnemonics view_fF Character Set Form feed character in View mode. View Mode Character Mnemonics view_frame Character Set Frame character in View mode. view_ht Character Set Horizontal tab character in View mode. view_menu Character Set Menu character. Copyright and Menu Mnemonics view_menu_check Character Set Appearance of a checked menu item in character mode. Character Mode Widget Mnemonics view_menu_cascade Character Set Indicator of a cascading menu item in character mode. view_mid Character Set Entity middle line in View mode. View Mode Character Mnemonics view_moredown Character Set ‘More below’ indicator in character mode. Character Mode Widget Mnemonics view_moreup Character Set ‘More above’ indicator in character mode. view_push_null Character Set Default text for a command button in character mode. view_radio Character Set Appearance for a radio button when on in character mode. view_radio_off Character Set Appearance for a radio button when off in character mode. view_top Character Set Entity top line in View mode. View Mode Character Mnemonics view_vert Character Set Entity left or right vertical line in View mode. Related Links Parameters in the Output Sequence Some mnemonics include parameters in the output sequence that can be used to determine layout and formatting information, such as horizontal and vertical coordinates, numeric base zero (decimal, octal, or hexadecimal), zero padding, and so on. The output sequence can even include expressions that use the parameters incalculation, for example, to increment values. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
